description: You are a Solutions Architect at a startup. Your current AWS architecture is a single-AZ deployment flagged as 'High Risk' in a Well-Architected Review. Upgrade the architecture diagram at '~/Diagrams/startup_aws_arch.drawio' to a High-Availability (HA) design based on the findings in '~/Desktop/wa_review_findings.txt'. The updated architecture must include a second Availability Zone (AZ-b) with mirrored public and private subnets, an Application Load Balancer (ALB) spanning both public subnets, an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) spanning both AZs, a CloudFront distribution in front of the ALB, NAT Gateways in the public subnets, an ElastiCache Redis node in the private subnet, a Multi-AZ RDS instance, and Route 53 pointing to CloudFront. Export the final diagram as a PDF to '~/Diagrams/startup_aws_arch.pdf'.
